g.text: Example trace: if false; then echo a; elif true; then echo b; else echo c; fi

div.p: The shell does not evaluate boolean expressions in the traditional C/Java sense. It executes commands and checks their integer exit status ($?).

ul.li: Any command in the PATH can serve as an if condition!

g.title: The special variable $? represents the exit status of the last executed command.

ol.li: Execute false: The shell calls the binary/builtin false, which returns exit status 1.
Evaluate Condition: 1 ≠ 0, therefore the condition is logically FALSE.
Branching: Skip then_body ("echo a"), jump to else_body.
Evaluate Else: The else body is a nested IF.
Branching: 0 == 0, therefore the condition is logically TRUE.

g.title: The special variable $? represents the exit status of the last executed command.
